notes: Fifth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school offers a complete education through an integrated curriculum centered around visual and performing arts, using an experiential approach.
The school says it uses a family-centered, cooperative approach that encourages parental involvement and community service to nurture responsible citizenship.
Its Four Pillars are Effort, Respect, Leadership, and Service.
Official materials say the school is part of the A+ Schools Program at the North Carolina Arts Council.
The PACE board page says the school is governed by Partnership for Art at the Core of Education, Inc.
Sarena Fuller is listed as Executive Director, and the school notes she was the 2024 North Carolina Wells Fargo Charter School Principal of the Year.
Official quick facts say the school opened its doors on August 22, 2013.
Its charter focus is project-based learning.
The school describes project-based learning as learning by doing through hands-on, real-world projects that mirror challenges beyond school.
Official materials say rigorous project-based learning at the school is organized around an open-ended driving question.
The board page says regular board meetings are held at 6:30 p.m.
Joey Johnson is listed in the school's quick facts as Executive Director, and Benjamin Averette is listed as board chair.
Its mission says the school is a community-initiated, environmentally conscious school guided by the Core Principles of Public Waldorf Education.
The curriculum page says instruction is aligned with the North Carolina Standard Course of Study and intentionally designed to honor the diverse cultural backgrounds of students.
A school leadership page describes the school as dedicated to whole-child development, equity, and lifelong learning.
Its board page says the board also holds quarterly strategy meetings in addition to monthly board meetings.
Official staff listings name Brittany Wheaton as Executive Director and Principal.
The board page lists Rich Antoine as chair and treasurer.
The homepage says the school's goal is to help parents develop exemplary young citizens with superior academic preparation, analytical thinking skills, a passion for learning, and virtuous character.
Its about page says charter schools exist to offer students, parents, and educators choices of teaching and learning methods and environments, with an emphasis on innovation and accountability.
The school says its curriculum is challenging and demanding in an age-appropriate manner and that its standards are high.
Its board page says the board is elected by parents of currently enrolled students.
Official governance information says board meetings are held on the third Thursday of each month at 6:30 p.m. in the Media Center.
Jeff Dishmon is listed as principal on the administration and office staff page.
Its mission says the school cultivates scholars for impactful global engagement, ethical citizenship, and lifelong learning.
Official school descriptions say advanced coursework in Greek language and cultural heritage forms the foundation of its intellectual and multicultural competencies.
The school says it applies the Socratic method to promote inquiry, logic, questioning, problem-solving, and team collaboration.
Its published core values include Filotimo, Arete, Philoxenia, Ethos, and Personal Responsibility.
The board page says monthly board meetings are held on the second Wednesday at 7:30 p.m.
The leadership page lists Stefanie Savage as Interim Head of School.
